lessons.title: Learn: Disk and Storage Management
lessons.content_md:
# Lab 08: Disk and Storage Management

Disk and storage management is about understanding how space is used, how to inspect storage devices, and how to organize files safely.

## Learning Objectives

- Check filesystem capacity with `df`
- Measure directory and file usage with `du`
- Inspect block devices with `lsblk`
- Archive and restore data with `tar`
- Resize and verify files safely with `truncate` and `stat`

## Core Topics

### 1) Filesystem Capacity

```bash
df -h
df -h /
```

### 2) Directory Space Analysis

```bash
du -sh ~/lab8
du -ah ~/lab8 | sort -h | tail -n 10
```

### 3) Device Visibility

```bash
lsblk
```

### 4) Archiving and Recovery

```bash
tar -czf backup.tar.gz folder/
tar -xzf backup.tar.gz
```

### 5) Safe Storage Habits

- Check free space before creating large files
- Use archives for transfer and backup workflows
- Verify size after resize operations
- Avoid deleting unknown system files

After Learn, complete command practice, then exercises, then MCQ.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 1 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 1

Create lab data and inspect usage.

```bash
cd ~ && mkdir -p ~/lab8/storageA ~/lab8/storageB
dd if=/dev/zero of=~/lab8/storageA/file1.bin bs=1K count=64 status=none && echo FILE1_CREATED
truncate -s 2M ~/lab8/storageA/file2.img && echo FILE2_CREATED
du -sh ~/lab8/storageA
df -h /
lsblk | head -n 10
find ~/lab8 -type f | wc -l
du -ah ~/lab8/storageA | sort -h | tail -n 5
mkdir -p ~/lab8/archive && cp ~/lab8/storageA/file1.bin ~/lab8/archive/
ln -s ~/lab8/storageA/file2.img ~/lab8/storageB/file2-link.img && echo SYMLINK_OK
```

tasks.description: C01: Create lab8 storage directories.
C02: Create file1.bin (64KB) and print marker FILE1_CREATED.
tasks.hint: Type: dd if=/dev/zero of=~/lab8/storageA/file1.bin bs=1K count=64 status=none && echo FILE1_CREATED

tasks.description: C03: Create file2.img (2MB) and print marker FILE2_CREATED.
C04: Show total size of storageA directory.
C05: Show filesystem usage for root mount.
C06: Show block device overview.
C07: Count files inside lab8.
C08: Display largest entries inside storageA.
C09: Copy file1.bin to archive directory.
C10: Create symbolic link for file2.img and print marker.

lessons.title: Command Practice Part 2 (10 Commands)
lessons.content_md:
## Guided Commands: Part 2

Practice archive, restore, and resize workflows.

```bash
tar -czf ~/lab8/storageA.tar.gz -C ~/lab8 storageA && echo TAR_CREATED
ls -lh ~/lab8/storageA.tar.gz
mkdir -p ~/lab8/restore && tar -xzf ~/lab8/storageA.tar.gz -C ~/lab8/restore && echo TAR_EXTRACTED
du -sh ~/lab8/restore/storageA
find ~/lab8 -type f -name '*.bin'
stat ~/lab8/storageA/file1.bin | head -n 5
truncate -s 1M ~/lab8/storageA/file2.img && echo RESIZED
du -sh ~/lab8/storageA
rm -f ~/lab8/archive/file1.bin && echo ARCHIVE_FILE_REMOVED
find ~/lab8 -maxdepth 2 -type d | sort
```

tasks.description: C11: Create compressed archive storageA.tar.gz and print marker.
C12: Show archive file details in human readable format.
C13: Extract archive into restore folder and print marker.
C14: Show size of restored storageA directory.
C15: Find .bin files inside lab8.
C16: Inspect metadata of file1.bin.
C17: Resize file2.img to 1MB and print RESIZED marker.
C18: Recheck total size of storageA after resize.
C19: Remove copied archive file and print marker.
C20: List lab8 directories up to depth 2.

lessons.title: Exercises (5)
lessons.content_md:
## Practical Exercises

Complete all exercises to prove disk and storage management skills.

- E1-E2: easy
- E3-E4: medium
- E5: medium-hard

tasks.description: E1: Create ~/lab8/ex1 with 3 files (128K, 256K, 512K) and print EX1_READY.
tasks.hint: Type: mkdir -p ~/lab8/ex1 && truncate -s 128K ~/lab8/ex1/a.dat && truncate -s 256K ~/lab8/ex1/b.dat && truncate -s 512K ~/lab8/ex1/c.dat && echo EX1_READY

tasks.description: E2: Create ex1 archive ex1.tar.gz and print EX1_ARCHIVED.
E3: Extract ex1.tar.gz into ~/lab8/ex1-restore and print EX1_RESTORED.
E4: Resize ~/lab8/ex1/a.dat to 64K and confirm SIZE_64K_OK.
E5: Show total size of ~/lab8/ex1 and print marker EX1_SIZE_DONE.

lessons.title: MCQ Quiz (5)
lessons.content_md:
## MCQ: Disk and Storage Management

Answer using option index (0-3). Example: submit 2.

tasks.description: Q1: Which command shows free and used disk space by filesystem?
Q2: Which command summarizes directory size usage?
Q3: Which command is used to list block devices?
Q4: Which command creates a compressed tar archive?
tasks.validation_rule: ["tar -xzf backup.tar.gz","gzip file.txt","zip -r backup.zip dir","tar -czf backup.tar.gz dir"]

tasks.description: Q5: Before deleting large files, what is best practice?
tasks.validation_rule: ["Delete first, inspect later","Verify path and impact before removal","Always use sudo rm -rf","Reboot system to clear space"]
